(西多摩郡, *Nishitama-gun*; West Tama) is a [district](/wiki/Districts_of_Japan "Districts of Japan") located in [Tokyo Metropolis](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"), [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"). It comprises the village of [Hinohara](/wiki/Hinohara,_Tokyo "Hinohara, Tokyo") and the following three towns:
["都立農林高校のトップページ"](https://web.archive.org/web/20080222070359/http://www.norin-h.metro.tokyo.jp/). Archived from [the original](http://www.norin-h.metro.tokyo.jp/) on 2008-02-22. Retrieved 2008-02-11.

(Redirected from [Nishitama District, Tokyo](/w/index.php?title=Nishitama_District,_Tokyo&redirect=no "Nishitama District, Tokyo"))

**Nishitama** (西多摩郡, *Nishitama-gun*; West Tama) is a [district](/wiki/Districts_of_Japan "Districts of Japan") located in [Tokyo Metropolis](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"), [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"). It comprises the village of [Hinohara](/wiki/Hinohara,_Tokyo "Hinohara, Tokyo") and the following three towns:

* [Hinode](/wiki/Hinode,_Tokyo "Hinode, Tokyo")
* [Mizuho](/wiki/Mizuho,_Tokyo "Mizuho, Tokyo")
* [Okutama](/wiki/Okutama,_Tokyo "Okutama, Tokyo")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d3/Kanagawa_NishiTama-gun_1889.png/250px-Kanagawa_NishiTama-gun_1889.png)](/wiki/File:Kanagawa_NishiTama-gun_1889.png)

Former boundaries of Nishitama District

Historically, the cities of [Ōme](/wiki/%C5%8Cme,_Tokyo "Ōme, Tokyo"), [Fussa](/wiki/Fussa,_Tokyo "Fussa, Tokyo"), [Hamura](/wiki/Hamura,_Tokyo "Hamura, Tokyo"), and [Akiruno](/wiki/Akiruno,_Tokyo "Akiruno, Tokyo") were parts of Nishitama District but these were broken off from the district after they were elevated to city status.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nishitama_District&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Education")]

Each of the towns and villages operates separate public elementary and junior high school systems.

[Tokyo Metropolitan Government Board of Education](/wiki/Tokyo_Metropolitan_Government_Board_of_Education "Tokyo Metropolitan Government Board of Education") operates [Mizuho Nougei High School](/wiki/Mizuho_Nougei_High_School "Mizuho Nougei High School")[[1]](#cite_note-1) in Mizuho.

The [school district](/wiki/School_district "School district") also operates the following high schools in nearby Ōme:

* [Norin High School](/w/index.php?title=Norin_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Norin High School (page does not exist)")[[2]](#cite_note-2)
* [Ome Sogo High School](/w/index.php?title=Ome_Sogo_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ome Sogo High School (page does not exist)")[[3]](#cite_note-3)
* [Tama High School](/w/index.php?title=Tama_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tama High School (page does not exist)")[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The district operates the following high schools in Fussa:

* [Fussa High School](/w/index.php?title=Fussa_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fussa High School (page does not exist)")[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* [Tama Technical High School](/w/index.php?title=Tama_Technical_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tama Technical High School (page does not exist)")[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The district operates [Hamura High School](/w/index.php?title=Hamura_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hamura High School (page does not exist)")[[7]](#cite_note-7) in Hamura.

The district operates the following schools in Akiruno:

* [Akirudai High School](/w/index.php?title=Akirudai_High_School&action=edit&redlink=1 "Akirudai High School (page does not exist)")[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* Itsukaichi High School[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## District timeline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nishitama_District&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: District timeline")]

* July 22, 1878: [Tama District](/w/index.php?title=Tama_District,_Musashi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tama District, Musashi (page does not exist)"), which it had long been part of former [Musashi Province](/wiki/Musashi_Province "Musashi Province"), was divided into four parts: [Higashitama](/w/index.php?title=Higashitama_District,_Tokyo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Higashitama District, Tokyo (page does not exist)") (Eastern Tama), [Kitatama](/wiki/Kitatama_District,_Tokyo "Kitatama District, Tokyo") (Northern Tama), [Minamitama](/wiki/Minamitama_District,_Tokyo "Minamitama District, Tokyo") (Southern Tama) and Nishitama (Western Tama). Nishitama District had 1 town and 93 village areas.
* April 1, 1889: The new legislation, Nishitama District had 2 towns and 30 villages areas.
* April 1, 1893: Nishitama District was transferred from [Kanagawa Prefecture](/wiki/Kanagawa_Prefecture "Kanagawa Prefecture") to [Tokyo Prefecture](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo").
* June 1, 1955: The villages of Ohisano and Hirai were merged to create the new village of Hinode.
* October 1, 1956: The village of Nishitama was elevated to town status and renamed to Hamura.
* October 15, 1958: Parts of the village of Motosayama (from [Iruma District](/wiki/Iruma_District,_Saitama "Iruma District, Saitama"), [Saitama Prefecture](/wiki/Saitama_Prefecture "Saitama Prefecture")), excluding the parts that was merged into the town of [Musashi](/wiki/Iruma,_Saitama "Iruma, Saitama") (from [Iruma District](/wiki/Iruma_District,_Saitama "Iruma District, Saitama")) on October 14, 1958, was merged into the town of [Mizuho](/wiki/Mizuho,_Tokyo "Mizuho, Tokyo").
* July 1, 1970: The town of Fussa was elevated to city status.
* May 5, 1972: The town of Akita was elevated to city status and renamed to Akigawa.
* June 1, 1974: The village of Hinode was elevated to town status.
* November 1, 1991: The town of Hamura was elevated to city status.
* September 1, 1995: The town of [Itsukaichi](/wiki/Itsukaichi,_Tokyo "Itsukaichi, Tokyo") was merged with the city of [Akigawa](/wiki/Akigawa,_Tokyo "Akigawa, Tokyo") to create the city of [Akiruno](/wiki/Akiruno,_Tokyo "Akiruno, Tokyo").
